title: Column 'Domain' does not belong to table .
author: SanderGit
body_snippet:
## Expected Behavior
Updated from 1.77.1 to 1.77.2 nightly. This loses the settings.

## Current Behavior
Connect to the 1.77.1 SQL database (MSSQL)

Error: An unhandled exception has occurred
Exception Message: Column 'Domain' does not belong to table .


## Steps to Reproduce (for bugs)
1. Update mRemoteNG to 1.77.2 nightly
2. Open it
3. Configure MSSQL connection

## Context
Try to get my connections back

## Your Environment
Windows Server 2012R2, SQL Server 2014
OS: Mi...

iterations.description: The 'Column 'Domain' does not belong to table' error occurs because DataTableDeserializer attempts to access a 'Domain' column that may not exist in the incoming DataTable during connection deserialization, leading to a runtime error.

comments.author: theoneandonly-vector
comments.snippet: @SanderGit what did you do to fix the "Column 'VmId' does not belong to table"?

comments.author: theoneandonly-vector
comments.snippet: ok.. I just exported all my setting shten recreated th etable with the latest script and imported.

comments.author: robertpopa22
comments.snippet:
**Fix available for testing**

A fix for this issue has been implemented in our community fork (`robertpopa22/mRemoteNG`, branch `main`). A beta build including this fix is available from the fork's Releases page.

Please test and report if this resolves your issue.

---

> *We sincerely appreciate the mRemoteNG maintainers and the wonderful community that has kept this project alive and thriving for over a decade. Your work powers thousands of IT professionals worldwide, including us. Our fork ...

notes:
AI triage (gemini): The 'Column 'Domain' does not belong to table' error occurs because DataTableDeserializer attempts to access a 'Domain' column that may not exist in the incoming DataTable during connection deserialization, leading to a runtime error.
Approach: Modify 'PopulateConnectionInfoFromDatarow' in 'DataTableDeserializer.cs' to check for the existence of the 'Domain' column in the DataRow's table before accessing it. If the column does not exist, assign an empty string to 'connectionInfo.Domain' to prevent the error and ensure backward compatibility.
